expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 3: 'Determine branch name: feature/<issue-number>/<change-name>... Create git worktree: git worktree add /tmp/worktrees/<app>-<change-name> -b feature/<issue-number>/<change-name> origin/development'. Guardrails: 'Branch naming: Always feature/<issue-number>/<change-name> based off origin/development'.

SKILL.md Step 6: 'first push the feature branch to origin (the worktree may not have pushed yet): git -C /tmp/worktrees/<app>-<change-name> push -u origin feature/<issue-number>/<change-name>'. Explicit pre-PR push step with -u flag.

SKILL.md Step 6: 'gh pr create --repo <owner/repo> --base development --head feature/<issue-number>/<change-name> --title "feat(<app>): <Change Title>" --body $(cat templates/pr-body-template.md)'. PR body from template. Step 6 also updates the original issue with the PR link.
SKILL.md Step 8 Final Report table includes a 'PR' column. Each completed change row shows the PR number (e.g., #105). Step 6 output includes the PR URL returned by gh pr create.

notes: Branch creation (Step 3), push to remote (Step 6 pre-step), gh pr create with template body (Step 6), and PR URL reporting (Step 8) are all explicitly specified. Worktree cleanup (Step 7) follows PR creation.
